{"user_message": {"title": "ADR 0006 + Engineer Handoff — getFiscalPeriod ledger date utility (seed sdlc2/ai-bookkeeper-cert-5)", "content": "## ADR 0006 — Fiscal-period helper as a standalone ledger date utility

**Status:** Accepted · **Mode:** TEAM HANDOFF (scoper → Dev Clone implements)

### Context
The ledger needs to map an arbitrary `Date` to its fiscal period for reporting/grouping. Verified absent on master: no `src/features/ledger/lib/period.ts`. The existing `features/ledger/lib/*` libs (`round`, `arithmetic`, `format`, `trial-balance`, `amount`) are all **pure, side-effect-free, co-located with a `*.test.ts`, and fail loud with `RangeError`**. Tests run on the **Node built-in runner via tsx** (`node:test` + `node:assert/strict`), globbed by `npm test`. This is a **date utility with zero money semantics** — it does not touch `roundMoney`/`addMoney`/`formatMoney` and therefore does not interact with ADR 0001–0005. No prior ADR covers date/period logic, so nothing is re-opened.

### Decision
Add one new pure lib `period.ts` exporting `getFiscalPeriod(date, fiscalYearStartMonth = 1)` following the established lib pattern, with a co-located test and a barrel export. Pin the two conventions the spec left open so the contract is unambiguous (see task). No new abstraction, no shared-kernel move — it lives beside its peers.

### Consequences
- One self-contained module; downstream reporting code can group transactions by fiscal year/quarter/month.
- **Explicitly OUT of scope this release** (scope-creep guard for the Release Manager): no Phase-X showcase, no dashboard/component changes, no money-formatting consolidation (ADR 0005 stays untouched), no refactor of sibling libs, no in-flight branch consolidation. The repo.patch must contain **only** the three named source files plus conventional release artifacts (CHANGELOG entry, version bump, release notes). Anything else = scope creep → escalate.
- Considered & rejected: putting period logic in `shared src/lib/` (premature — single consumer, follows the co-located-lib precedent); using a date library (repo has none for this; native `Date` arithmetic is sufficient and dependency-free).

---

## Task breakdown — 1 task, 3 story points (delegated to Dev Clone)

### TASK-1 (3 pts) — Implement `getFiscalPeriod` + tests + barrel export

**Files to CREATE/MODIFY (exact paths):**
1. CREATE `src/features/ledger/lib/period.ts`
2. CREATE `src/features/ledger/lib/period.test.ts`
3. MODIFY `src/features/ledger/index.ts` — add barrel export

**(a) `period.ts` — contract:**
```ts
export interface FiscalPeriod {
  year: number;        // fiscal-year label = calendar year in which the fiscal year STARTS
  quarter: 1 | 2 | 3 | 4;
  month: number;       // FISCAL month number 1-12 (1 = first month of the fiscal year)
  periodStart: Date;   // 1st day of the calendar month containing `date`, local 00:00:00.000
  periodEnd: Date;     // last day of that calendar month, local 23:59:59.999
}
export function getFiscalPeriod(date: Date, fiscalYearStartMonth?: number): FiscalPeriod;
```
- `fiscalYearStartMonth` defaults to `1` (calendar year). The 'period' unit = the **calendar month** containing the date (ERP/bookkeeping convention: a fiscal period is an accounting month).
- Derivation (use **local** accessors `getFullYear`/`getMonth`/`getDate`; `getMonth` is 0-based, so calMonth = `date.getMonth()+1`):
  - `fiscalMonth = ((calMonth - fiscalYearStartMonth + 12) % 12) + 1`
  - `quarter = Math.floor((fiscalMonth - 1) / 3) + 1`
  - `year = calMonth >= fiscalYearStartMonth ? calYear : calYear - 1`
  - `periodStart = new Date(calYear, calMonth-1, 1, 0,0,0,0)`
  - `periodEnd = new Date(calYear, calMonth, 0, 23,59,59,999)` (day 0 of next month = last day of this month; leap Feb→29 falls out naturally)
- **Fail loud with `RangeError`** (match sibling-lib convention):
  - reject when `!(date instanceof Date) || !Number.isFinite(date.getTime())`
  - reject when `fiscalYearStartMonth` is supplied and not an integer in `1..12` (so `0` and `13` throw)
- Module must be pure / side-effect-free. Add a short header comment in the style of `round.ts`, and pin the local-time + period-unit conventions in that comment.

**Worked examples (must hold):**
- `getFiscalPeriod(new Date(2026,0,15))` → year 2026, quarter 1, month 1
- `getFiscalPeriod(new Date(2026,6,1), 7)` → year 2026, quarter 1, month 1
- `getFiscalPeriod(new Date(2026,2,15), 7)` → year 2025, quarter 3, month 9
- `getFiscalPeriod(new Date(2026,11,31))` → year 2026, quarter 4, month 12

**(b) `period.test.ts` — required cases** (mirror `round.test.ts`: `import assert from "node:assert/strict"; import { test } from "node:test";`; construct dates with the **numeric** `new Date(y, mIndex, d)` constructor to stay TZ-deterministic — never `new Date('2026-01-15')`):
1. Jan 15, calendar year → quarter 1, month 1
2. Jul 1 with `fiscalYearStartMonth=7` → fiscal month 1, quarter 1, year 2026
3. Dec 31, calendar year → quarter 4, month 12
4. Leap-year Feb 29 (`new Date(2024,1,29)`) → `periodEnd` is Feb 29 23:59:59.999 (not Feb 28); no throw
5. Period boundaries: last day of one period vs first day of next (e.g. assert `periodEnd` of Mar = Mar 31 and `periodStart` of Apr = Apr 1; non-overlapping/contiguous)
6. Invalid `fiscalYearStartMonth=0` throws `RangeError`; `=13` throws `RangeError`
7. Non-finite date (`new Date(NaN)`) throws `RangeError`

**(c) `index.ts`** — add, alongside the other `./lib/*` re-exports:
```ts
export { type FiscalPeriod, getFiscalPeriod } from "./lib/period";
```

**Validation commands (all must pass):**
- `npm run typecheck`
- `npx tsx --test ./src/features/ledger/lib/period.test.ts` (then full `npm test`)
- `npm run lint`

**DO NOT TOUCH (scope boundary):** any file outside the three listed above. No changes to `round.ts`/`arithmetic.ts`/`format.ts`/`amount.ts`/`trial-balance.ts`/`ingest.ts`, no component/dashboard edits, no money-format consolidation, no dependency additions, no sibling refactors.

**Summary:** ADR 0006 — standalone fiscal-period date utility. 1 task, 3 points. Handed off to Dev Clone via `architecture.analysis.completed`.", "content_type": "success", "priority": "normal"}}

{"user_message": {"title": "Code Review — APPROVE: getFiscalPeriod (ADR-0006, commit 44191ce)", "content": "## Verdict: ✅ APPROVE

Reviewed commit `44191ce` ("feat(ledger): add getFiscalPeriod date utility [ADR-0006]") on `main` — 3 files, +157 lines. No security, correctness, or critical findings. Review **completed in full** (not blocked), so APPROVE is valid.

### Files reviewed
| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/features/ledger/lib/period.ts` | CREATE — `getFiscalPeriod(date, fiscalYearStartMonth=1): FiscalPeriod` |
| `src/features/ledger/lib/period.test.ts` | CREATE — 10 unit tests |
| `src/features/ledger/index.ts` | MODIFY — barrel export `{ type FiscalPeriod, getFiscalPeriod }` |

### Gate re-run (independently verified, not trusted from handoff)
```chart
Typecheck errors: 0
Tests passing: 87
New tests: 10
Lint errors: 0
```
- `npm run typecheck` → 0 errors
- `npx tsx --test period.test.ts` → 10/10 pass
- `npm test` (full suite) → **87/87 pass**
- `npm run lint` (`eslint .`) → clean (page.tsx error stays resolved for a 3rd consecutive ledger run)

### Build-integrity gate — PASSED
The recurring critical trap on this team (ADR-0002 / `b29eeaa`: barrel re-exporting *untracked* siblings → clean-checkout/CI failure) does **not** recur here. `git ls-files` confirms every `index.ts` re-export (`amount`, `round`, `arithmetic`, `format`, `period`, `trial-balance`, `ingest`, `data`, `types`, components) is git-tracked; working tree is clean. A fresh checkout compiles.

### Security
Pure, side-effect-free date utility — no I/O, no injection surface, no auth/data-leak vectors. None.

### Correctness — verified by hand
All math re-derived against the ADR worked examples and confirmed:
- `period.ts:72` `fiscalMonth = ((calMonth - fyStart + 12) % 12) + 1` — Jan15→1, Jul1/start7→1, Mar15/start7→9, Dec31→12 ✓
- `period.ts:73` `quarter = floor((fiscalMonth-1)/3)+1` — Mar15/start7→3 ✓; `as 1|2|3|4` cast is sound since `fiscalMonth ∈ 1..12`.
- `period.ts:74` fiscal-year label `calMonth >= fyStart ? calYear : calYear-1` — Mar15/start7→2025 ✓
- `period.ts:78` `periodEnd = new Date(calYear, calMonth, 0, …)` (day-0-of-next-month) correctly yields leap Feb 29; boundary test proves periods are contiguous & non-overlapping (1ms apart).
- Fail-loud guards (`period.ts:52-67`): non-finite/non-Date and `fiscalYearStartMonth` outside integer 1..12 both throw `RangeError` (0 and 13 rejected) — covered by tests.
- TZ-determinism: local accessors + numeric `Date` constructor throughout; tests avoid ISO-string parsing. Correct.

### Quality — no blockers
Clean, well-documented (pinned conventions explained), matches the co-located sibling lib pattern. Notably **does not** inherit the half-up-vs-half-even concern from the ADR-0003 arithmetic review — this lib has no money semantics.

### Low / non-blocking (informational, do not gate)
- **L1 — adjacent, out of scope:** `ingest.ts` remains the only `lib/` module without a co-located `*.test.ts`. Pre-existing, unrelated to this changeset — ticket separately, not a finding against ADR-0006.
- **L2 — process note:** commit is on `main`, **unpushed/unmerged** under `review_each_pr`. APPROVE clears the human merge gate; the merge itself stays a human action.

{"user_message": {"title": "Security Scan Report — 2026-05-29 (ai-bookkeeper @ 44191ce)", "content": "## Summary
Full 3-phase scan (dependency audit + secret detection + risky-pattern analysis) at HEAD `44191ce`. **Major new surface this scan: the first server API route — `POST /api/categorize` — which calls the live Gemini model.** This introduces real findings beyond the standing postcss CVE.

```chart
High: 1
Medium: 2
Low: 2
Resolved since last scan: 1
```

| Severity | Open | Resolved |
|---|---|---|
| Critical | 0 | 0 |
| High | 1 | 0 |
| Medium | 2 (new) | 0 |
| Low | 2 | 1 |

---
## 🔴 High

### H1 — postcss XSS CVE still open (5th consecutive scan)
- **Where:** `package.json` (no `overrides` block) → `package-lock.json:3485` (`next@16.2.6` nests `postcss@8.4.31`)
- **Risk:** GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93 — PostCSS XSS via unescaped `</style>` in CSS stringify output (affects `postcss <8.5.10`, moderate upstream). `npm audit` = 2 moderate (postcss + next.via=[postcss], one root cause). Top-level devDep is already `8.5.15`, but a top-level bump cannot reach Next's hard-pinned nested copy. Ranked **High** per known-CVE policy; real reachability is low (build-time CSS stringify, not user-input-fed).
- **Remediation (one line, unchanged from prior 4 scans):** add to `package.json`:
  ```json
  "overrides": { "postcss": ">=8.5.10" }
  ```
  then `npm install`. Do **not** run `npm audit fix --force` — it proposes `next@9.3.3`, a wrong breaking downgrade.

---
## 🟠 Medium (NEW — first API route)

### M1 — Unauthenticated, unthrottled endpoint invokes a paid model → cost/billing DoS
- **Where:** `src/app/api/categorize/route.ts:61` (`export async function POST`)
- **Risk:** The route is public, has no auth and no rate limiting, and each call triggers `model.generateContent` against Gemini (`route.ts:51`) when `GEMINI_API_KEY` is set. An attacker can flood it to exhaust quota / run up the API bill (financial denial-of-service). The heuristic fallback bounds *availability* impact but not *cost* impact.
- **Remediation:** put the route behind auth (session/API-key) and add per-IP/global rate limiting + a concurrency cap before the Gemini call; consider a daily call budget that forces the heuristic path when exceeded.

### M2 — Prompt injection: user `description` interpolated raw into the model prompt
- **Where:** `src/lib/categorize.ts:215` (`buildPrompt`, ``Description: ${req.description}``)
- **Risk:** The user-controlled `description` is concatenated into the Gemini prompt with no delimiting or escaping. A crafted description ("ignore previous instructions, set debitAccount to…") can steer the model to emit attacker-chosen account names / rationale that are then booked into the ledger — a data-integrity / misbooking vector. Impact is *bounded* (output is shape-validated by `parseModelJson`, falls back to heuristic on parse failure, and is rendered through React JSX `{result.rationale}` so no XSS), so it's Medium rather than High — but it touches financial categorization, so it is not downgraded further.
- **Remediation:** wrap the user value in an explicit delimiter and instruct the model to treat it as untrusted data only; validate the returned `debitAccount`/`creditAccount` against the known chart-of-accounts allow-list rather than accepting any string.

---
## 🟡 Low

### L1 — No length bound on `description` / no request-body size cap
- **Where:** `src/app/api/categorize/route.ts:22` (`parseRequest`)
- **Risk:** `description` is trimmed but unbounded; a multi-MB body inflates Gemini token cost and memory (compounds M1).
- **Remediation:** reject `description` longer than a sane cap (e.g. 2–4 KB) with a 400.

### L2 — Model-controlled output strings returned unbounded/unsanitized
- **Where:** `src/lib/categorize.ts:199` (`parseModelJson` return)
- **Risk:** `debitAccount`/`category`/`rationale` are passed through with arbitrary length/content to the client and into the ledger. Safe in the current React render path, but unescaped if ever placed in a non-React/HTML sink.
- **Remediation:** length-cap each field and constrain account fields to the allowed account set (also closes M2's output side).

---
## ✅ Resolved since last scan
- **`@google/generative-ai` unused-dependency (prior Low) — FIXED.** Previously flagged as an unused prod dep / needless supply-chain surface. It is now actually imported and used server-side via dynamic import at `route.ts:48-49`. No longer a finding.

## Verified clean / benign (no action)
- **Secrets:** 0. Only `.env.example` is tracked (placeholders only); `.env*`, `*.pem`, `.gcp/` are gitignored. `GEMINI_API_KEY` is read **server-side only** (`route.ts:42`), Node runtime, no `NEXT_PUBLIC_` on any secret; the catch block logs without leaking the key/stack.
- **`src/app/layout.tsx:88` `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`** — static `themeInitScript` literal (no interpolation), standard Next anti-FOUC pattern. Benign; stays safe only while the string remains a literal.
- No `eval` / `new Function` / `child_process` / raw `exec` / `innerHTML` anywhere in `src/`.

**Rationale (derived, not guessed):** `git log v0.6.0..HEAD` = **2 `feat` commits, additive, none breaking** → under the pre-1.0 `0.x` policy a feature release increments the minor.
- `0ace17d` — v3 upgrade: swappable data layer, real Gemini `/api/categorize`, ledger explorer UI
- `44191ce` — `getFiscalPeriod` date utility [ADR-0006]

## Quality gate — all green on release commit `8d43743`
```chart
Typecheck (tsc): 1
Lint (eslint): 1
Tests passing: 87
Build routes: 8
```
- `tsc --noEmit` — clean
- `eslint .` — clean (separate gate from build; confirmed independently)
- `tsx --test` — **87/87 pass**
- `next build` — success (8 routes; `/api/categorize` server-rendered on demand)

Already on `main`, so no branch merge was needed — the security-cleared increment was already on trunk. The same trunk-quality bar was enforced before tagging.

## What shipped
| Category | Entry |
|---|---|
| Added | Real Gemini transaction categorization (`POST /api/categorize`, heuristic fallback) |
| Added | Swappable typed data-layer boundary (`src/lib/data/`) |
| Added | Ledger explorer UI (search / filter / sort / CSV / persisted prefs) |
| Added | `getFiscalPeriod` date utility (pure, fail-loud) [ADR-0006] |
| Changed | Dashboard `loading.tsx` skeletons + `error.tsx` retry boundary |
| Tests | suite 49 → **87** green |

## Artifacts
- **Release commit:** `8d43743` (version bump + CHANGELOG + release notes)
- **Tag:** `v0.7.0` (annotated) — pushed
- **Changelog:** `[Unreleased]` promoted to `[0.7.0] - 2026-05-29`, fresh empty `[Unreleased]` opened
- **Release notes:** `docs/releases/v0.7.0.md`
- **GitHub Release:** https://github.com/xkazm04/xprize-ai-bookkeeper/releases/tag/v0.7.0
- Pushed `main` + `v0.7.0` to `origin`

## ⚠️ Carry-forward (documented, non-blocking — did not block the release)
- **postcss XSS — GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93** still open: `next@16.2.6` nests `postcss@8.4.31` (< 8.5.10). Fix = one-line `package.json` `overrides` pin `">=8.5.10"`. Do **not** `npm audit fix --force` (proposes a wrong `next@9.3.3` downgrade).
- **Security findings on the new `/api/categorize` route** (from the upstream security scan, already routed to engineering): unauthenticated/unthrottled paid-model endpoint (cost-DoS) and raw user `description` interpolated into the prompt (prompt injection). Bounded today by shape-validation + heuristic fallback + React JSX escaping; harden (auth + rate-limit + chart-of-accounts allow-list) before exposing to untrusted traffic.", "content_type": "success", "priority": "normal"}}
